courseleaf.title: Entomology, Master of Science
courseleaf.informationtext:
Graduate Study

The Department of Department of Entomology & Nematology offers a program of study and research leading to M.S. and Ph.D. degrees. For further details, see Graduate Studies.

Degree Requirements & Program Coordinator

See Entomology.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Entomology programs emphasize the integration of basic and applied aspects of arthropod sciences in solving problems in biology, agriculture, medicine, and the environment. Facilities at Davis provide resources for a wide range of studies for our Entomology graduate students. In addition to our state of the art laboratories, equipment, controlled environmental growth chambers, agricultural plots, and greenhouses, Davis is home to the Richard M. Bohart Museum of Entomology [https://bohart.ucdavis.edu/] and the Harry H. Laidlaw Jr. Honey Bee Research Center [https://beebiology.ucdavis.edu/].
